twitter_thread: 73% of AI teams use closed-source software, despite FOSS alternatives like OpenNLP outperforming them by 25%.
OpenNLP's maximum entropy tagging can process 10,000 tokens/second, beating some paid tools.
TensorFlow and PyTorch dominate 85% of machine learning deployments, and they're both FOSS.
Not reading FOSS documentation can cost you 3x in development time, as seen with spaCy's entity recognition.
SpaCy's NER model achieves 95% accuracy, making it a staple in many AI workflows.
FOSS apps like NLTK and Gensim offer 90% of the functionality of paid tools, with full customization.
What's the most surprising advantage you've found with FOSS apps in AI development? #foss #ai
pack.linkedin: I've found that 73% of AI teams use closed-source software, despite FOSS alternatives like OpenNLP offering 25% better performance. For example, OpenNLP's maximum entropy tagging can process 10,000 tokens per second, outpacing some paid tools. TensorFlow and PyTorch, which dominate 85% of machine learning deployments, are also FOSS. However, not reading the documentation for FOSS apps like spaCy can triple development time. SpaCy's NER model achieves 95% accuracy, and FOSS apps like NLTK and Gensim offer 90% of the functionality of paid tools, with full customization. What's your experience with using FOSS apps in AI development?
tiktok_hooks: Did you know 73% of AI teams are using the wrong software?
FOSS apps can give you a 25% performance boost
You're probably wasting time with closed-source software
pack.reddit_headline: 73% of AI teams are using closed-source software, but FOSS is 25% better - can anyone justify the closed-source choice?
